mechanism_narrative: A consistently rising industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing output and economic strength, which supports corporate earnings and lifts equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises by more than 1% over a quarter and the 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 40% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5% indicates a tight labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5%, the median 20-day S&P 500 return is negative or not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a high federal funds rate signals an aggressive inflation-fighting monetary stance that contracts valuations and depresses equity prices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than half of occurrences or the average return exceeds 1%.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, elevated housing starts represent a topping signal as residential investment saturates, leading to a subsequent decline in equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: During late-cycle regimes (GDP growth above 3% for two consecutive quarters), high HOUST levels are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A very high level of nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor demand and income growth, which sustains consumer spending and lifts equity prices rapidly within a week.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS exceeds 135,000, the 5-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth reflects the overall expansion of the economy and directly boosts corporate profitability, leading to positive equity returns over a three-month horizon regardless of transitory noise.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 increases quarter-over-quarter and the 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 30% of such quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production accelerates while unemployment declines, the economy is operating near capacity with synchronized strength, which amplifies positive equity momentum over a 20-day window.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > previous 3-month moving average and UNRATE < previous 3-month moving average
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of occurrences or the mean return is less than 0%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates a restrictive monetary policy that raises the cost of capital and discount rates, dragging down equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of cases or the average return exceeds 0.5%.
